Legendary Ranger

The world is larger than most people ever see. They are
born in a village, and they may die in a city, if they are
adventurous. Everything in between is a road
connecting known civilizations. The Ranger has spent
their life in the space those roads never reach–the
deep wilderness, the unmapped mountains, the
forests that stretch beyond the horizon. They know what
lives there, among the unknown. They know how it
thinks. And after twenty levels of hunting the most
dangerous game the Universe has to offer, they’ve
crowned themselves the legitimate champions of vast,
inconceivable lands, where creatures powerful and
great have learned to fear and respect the
Ranger’s step.

A Legendary Ranger has become the apex
of the hunting grounds and earned that place
one encounter at a time. These individuals are
slayers of peerless skill, whose instincts and
capacities far exceed those of any beast. They
move through terrains forgotten and uncharted
with the ease of someone who was never a
stranger to it.

The Ranger has learned to inhabit the
world completely, to think with its own vast logic,
to use its geography the way other warriors may use
armor or weapons. They have tracked quarry across
planar boundaries. They have survived conditions
that would kill anything less dedicated, less prepared,
less stubborn. When a Legendary Ranger sets their
sights on something, their relentless spirit carries them
forward as steadfastly as an arrow cuts through air.

Now, beyond 20th level, their quarry may well be
civilized–divine, even. But the sight of the Legendary
Ranger’s bow being tensed, of the impassive, magicinundated red eyes behind it… will make them quiver
like a cornered beast nonetheless.

Legendary Rogue

Somewhere in the foundations of empires, holy orders,
and great noble houses, there is always a task that must
be done covertly, kept separate from the legitimate
circles of society, yet as vital as any politician’s speech
or warlord’s sword. Something, often times unseemly or
even dishonorable, must be done about a document, a
truth, a person. Someone with a unique set of skills and
a particular relationship with visibility must be called to
act under the shadows.

A Legendary Rogue has been that someone for their
entire lives, and the secrets they carry could end
dynasties. They have moved through the courts of kings
and the dens of demon lords with equal fluency, leaving
no trace except the consequences of their passing–
which are always significant, and while too great to be a
coincidence, too perfectly orchestrated for anyone to
suspect their hand.

The great powers of the Universe announce
themselves. The rogue, on the other hand, has built an
entire philosophy around the opposite, and above 20th
level, that subtelty has become an invisible blade upon
the throats of all who interest them. A promise here, a
word there, and often, a blade in the dark; these are the
tools of a power unseen and unheard, always under the
surface until the precise moment to strike.
Proclamation begets resistance. True dominion is
silent.

At this tier, a Legendary Rogue is a supernaturally
dexterous, almost limitless being, making the
exceedingly unlikely seem natural. A dagger that finds
the gap in an all-knowing creature’s defense. A gaze that
unveils the nature of a hidden plot with the slimmest of
evidence. Reflexes so perfected, they weave through the
most perilous attacks and magical threats. Every act is
guided by a lifetime spent changing the course of
history, building the foundations of the present and the
future… without anyone knowing about it. The legacy of
a rogue does not include their name.

Legendary Sorcerer

There are many bloodlines in the Universe that can
produce mortals with innate magic–sorcerers, as the
scholars called them. A brush with an angel, a romance
with a devil, a draconic blessing… these things come
upon a person, and by the mysteries of magic, their
great-granddaughter is born with healing hands, or a
fiery breath. In their rarity, these things are fairly
common. You might find thousands of sorcerers in any
given nation. The bloodlines that produce Legendary
Sorcerers, on the other hand, are unique. There
probably isn’t another creature of their kind in living
memory. Of course, there is also effort–sorcery isn’t
only about the mass of the inheritance, but also the
conduit that channels it with more or less efficiency. A
Legendary Sorcerer is at the far end of both potential
and technique.

These mythical creatures are a shard of something
important and forgotten, now returned to its fullest
potential. Their power does not come from devotion or
discipline or decades of careful learning. It erupts, like a
volcano or a sudden storm, from somewhere inside
them, growing in intensity across 20 levels. And for
those of peerless achievement, it grows to reach what is
perhaps its final purpose–surpassment of the ancients.
Who’s to say a Legendary Sorcerer isn’t mightier than
the dragon who blessed their bloodline eons ago, as a
younger tree grows to tower over the one that spawned
its seed? Such a thing is difficult to ascertain, given the
antiquity in which such bloodline mutations may have
been imparted, but when a Legendary Sorcerer
devastates a fortress by making a volcano sprout from
its foundations, the truth of this question is revealed
without the need for hard evidence. The Legendary
Sorcerer has joined the mythical halls of those who
bless bloodlines, beyond the category of those who use
such blessings.

Greater Metamagic Options

You can use both a Metamagic and a Greater
Metamagic option in the same spell. You can only use
one Metamagic option on a single spell at a time. When
you reach 23rd level, this limitation is removed.

Dominant Spell: When you cast a spell with a duration
of Instantaneous, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to
make it impervious to abjuration. It cannot be
counterspelled and is not nullified by fields of anti-magic
or other similar magical effects of Higher tier or lower.
Only Epic tier magic can nullify a Dominant Spell.

Metamorphed Spell: When you deal damage with a
spell, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to change the
damage type to any other of your choice, as the matrix
of the spell transforms to suit your will.

Unendurable Spell: When you cast a spell that forces
a creature to make a saving throw to resist its effects,
you can expend 2 Legendary Points to make one target
of the spell fail its saving throw, regardless of the roll. A
target can still resist the effects of the spell by using a
Legendary Resistance or similar feature.

Overchanneled Spell: When you roll damage for a
spell, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to maximize
the result of all damage dice. If the spell is being cast at
7th level or higher, you must expend 2 Legendary
Points to use this Greater Metamagic Option instead.

Omnified Spell: When you cast a spell with an area of
effect, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to duplicate
its numerical range. A 30-foot cone, for example,
becomes a 60-foot cone, and a 5-foot wide, 30-foot long
line becomes a 10-foot wide, 60-foot long line.

Echoed Spell: When you cast a spell that targets only
one creature, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to
create a phantasmal echo of the spell that lingers
around the targeted creature for a minute. As a bonus
action, you can cast the spell again originating from the
target without expending a spell slot. You can have only
one Echoed Spell active at a time. To be eligible, a spell
must be of 9th level or lower and incapable of targeting
more than one creature at the spell’s current level. For
example, magic missile and scorching ray aren’t
eligible, but suggestion and dominate monster are.

Incapacitating Spell. When you cast a spell that
applies the stunned, paralyzed, frightened, or charmed
condition, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to make it
affect creatures even if they are immune to those
conditions.

Legendary Warlock

Every deal has two sides. They are not always born out
of desperation; a moment of weakness in the night,
where someone gives something precious for something
binding. Sometimes, a deal merely represents two
parties recognizing, with perfect clarity, that they need
each other. A patron reaches across the vast and
howling dark not because a mortal begged, but because
their attention was piqued. And, in the long history of
powers beyond mortal comprehension making bets on
mortal lives, some of these deals become much more
profitable than others.

After an entire life of adventuring, who exactly got the
better end of that deal?

A Legendary Warlock has spent their career in a
relationship that most people would find difficult to
explain. A constant negotiation with a being that doesn’t
always have their best interests in mind. The warlock
has delivered the expected results consistently,
across twenty levels of impossible situations,
and the power flowing through them reflects
that track record. They are no longer considered
a vassal, but a partner, and sometimes even an
equal. Whether the Patron has fully admitted it
yet or not, the balance of that partnership struck
at the beginning of the Warlock’s adventure has
shifted in ways that render the logic of servitude moot.

The few Legendary Warlocks that walk the cosmos
have come to alter their deal. They carry a power
intimate, built over struggle. Those who began as slaves
have come to the top and inverted their bonds. Those
who spent their lives pleasing their patron are now part
of that creature’s inner circle. Each relationship is
different, each unique.

At 20th level, the warlock has become the ultimate
representation of their patron’s power. A chosen
crusader, an unstoppable reaper, an endorsed overlord;
the power a Legendary Warlock commands has long
since outgrown the original terms of the agreement.
They have survived things their Patron did not
anticipate. Learned things their Patron did not intend to
teach. Arrived at a level of capability that places them,
whether either party planned for it, somewhere in the
vicinity of the beings they once struck a deal with.

Legendary Wizard

The Universe is full of unanswered questions.
Somewhere, a lowly student of the arcane chose to pick
up a quill and ponder their meaning, uncovering spells
of great power and utility as almost an afterthought to
the unpuzzling of reality. With courage and curiosity,
these bookworms left their libraries and walked the
world, recognizing that their field of study demands
practical knowledge. And thus, working tirelessly and
answering conundrums, they assembled a picture of
what magic truly means, in its very essence.

The culmination of twenty levels of patient work has
made these wizards into some of the greatest mortals
the cosmos has ever seen. Every arcanism is the
endpoint of a chain of reasoning that started with a
simple graphism inside a spellbook and ended with the
fiery explosions of meteors raining down. The arcane
reveals itself only to those whose minds are sharp
enough to decipher it, pouring through pages and pages
of scribbled notes and ink-drawn sigils. This hard and
methodical work has proven valuable beyond what
anyone would have expected of that determined student
who ventured out into the unknown, in search of
something most mortals don’t care about beyond a
superficial level: understanding.

Legendary Wizards are fascinating: the greatest
scientists, the worst villains, the wisest elders. The raw
power available to them at this tier is staggering, but it
is the precise and methodical personality behind it that
sets them apart from other spellcasters. A Wizard’s
abode might contain items of incredible power created
by the combination of hundreds of carefully devised
spells, great constructs of magic that defy the definition
of life, and alchemical formulas that result in diseases
more virulent than any natural pathogen, and stimulants
more potent than holy blessings.

A spellbook once empty now glows with the light of a
thousand runes. Within each page is a fragment of the
paradigm of understanding they have developed, a
theory of everything within which the behavior of both
the Universe and the Legendary Wizard can be
explained. When magic alights upon the palms of these
archmages, entire worlds shudder at the coming
arcanisms.

Level 20

Legendary Points

Starting at 20th level, you gain Legendary Points. Your
level determines the number of points you have, as
shown in the Legendary Points column of the
Legendary Wizard table. You can take the Recover
action on your turn to regain all expended Legendary
Points. You can spend these points to fuel various
features.

Legendary Resistance

Starting at 20th level, you can expend 1 Legendary
Point when you fail a saving throw to succeed instead.

Legendary AC

At 20th level, if your AC calculation is lower than your
level, you can use your level as your AC instead. If you
are using your Legendary AC, you cannot further
increase your AC in any way, except by temporary AC
increases or magical effects lasting 1 hour or less. Other
spells and effects that increase your AC only affect your
regular AC.

Legendary Arcanisms

Also at 20th level, you gain the ability to make spells you
cast more potent and harder to nullify. When you
expend a spell slot to cast a wizard spell, you can
expend 1 Legendary Point to upcast it as if you had
used a spell slot 3 levels higher than the spell slot you
expended, up to a 9th-level upcast. This way, a spell cast
at 1st level is instead cast at 4th level, and a spell cast at
6th level is instead cast at 9th level.

Level 21

Heroic Path Advancement

At 21st level, you choose a Heroic Path you meet the
prerequisites for and advance its first step. Each time
you gain a level, you can advance a Heroic Path by one
step, start a new Heroic Path, or, if specified as an
option of the current step you’re on, jump from one
Heroic Path into another.

Original PDF page 81

Legendary Score Improvement

When you reach 21st level, and again at levels 23 and
25, increase one of your ability scores by 2 or two ability
scores by 1. You cannot raise an ability score above 24
using this feature, unless another feature raised your
maximum for that specific ability score beforehand.

Sacrificial Conduits

Also at 21st level, you learn to fashion false prana
conduits that allow you to use spell slots you’ve already
spent, at the cost of your own health. You can cast a
spell even if you don’t have the required spell slot for it.
If you do so, you increase your exhaustion level by an
amount equal to one-third the level of the spell you cast,
rounded up (minimum of 1).

You can use this ability to cast a 10th-level spell, after
learning one. If you do so, you disintegrate into arcanely
charged dust at the end of your turn, using your very
essence to maintain such an arcanism. Only a Wish or
True Resurrection spell can bring you back to life.

Level 22

10th-Level Spell

At 22nd level, you learn a single 10th-level spell. You
learn an additional 10th-level spell at 24th level. When
you start casting a 10th-level spell, you must expend 2
Legendary Points alongside the required action and
spell slot.

Efficient Flow

Also at 22nd level, you learn to use your conduits with
near-perfect efficiency, allowing you to manifest spells
faster. You can cast various spells that expend spell
slots in the same turn, ignoring the usual restrictions for
spellcasting, and you can concentrate on one additional
spell at a time. If your concentration is broken, all spells
you were concentrating on end.

The number of spells you can concentrate on at a
time can never exceed 4, even if you gain this same
benefit from various sources.

Level 23

Omniturgical Possibility

At 23rd level, you learn to manifest magic that is
normally out of reach for wizards. You can expend 2
Legendary Points to cast a spell of 9th level or lower
without expending a spell slot. The spell can be one that
you don’t know or have prepared. Having witnessed the
spell being cast or having read about it is sufficient for
you to perform it. The spell doesn’t need to be a wizard
spell, but it counts as one for you. You must still abide
by all other rules of spellcasting.

Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until
you complete a long rest.

Greater Magicker

Also at 23rd level, your understanding of magic makes
your spells even harder to resist. Increase your Spell
Save DC by 2. Your magic always counts as Higher
magic, even if it is Lower, for the purposes of Tiers of
Magic.

Level 24

Arcane Resilience

Starting at 24th level, you have resistance to damage
from spells. If you already had resistance to damage
from spells from another source, you gain resistance to
damage from all magical effects instead.

Wizardly Recovery

Also at 24th level, when you use the Recover action, you
regain one spell slot of up to 5th level.

Memorized Spellbook

When you reach 24th level, your Spellbook has become
obsolete. You have memorized all the contents from
your Spellbook and no longer require it to cast Ritual
spells. You have all spells from your Spellbook prepared
at any time. Whenever you add a spell to your
Spellbook, you immediately memorize it and always
have it prepared.

You no longer require an arcane focus to cast spells,
using your mind as your arcane focus.

Level 25

Arcane Exemplar

At 25th level, you gain an additional 9th-level spell slot
(included on the class table), and you learn the ability to
concentrate on an additional spell at a time. If your
concentration is broken, all spells you were
concentrating on end.

The number of spells you can concentrate on at a time
can never exceed 4, even if you gain this same benefit
from various sources.
